MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar Jan-29-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Patrick R. Campbell of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Dale Stein and Jean Stein

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER ADVANCING CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for preference pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a) is granted. The May 18, 2015 trial date is maintained with preference status. All dates related to discovery, motions and disclosures remain dictated by the relevant provisions of the C.C.P. based on this trial date. Plaintiffs met the requirements of C.C.P. 36(a) by demonstrating that Mr. Stein is over 70, has a substantial interest in the action as a whole, and his health is such that preference status is necessary to prevent prejudicing his interest in the litigation. However plaintiffs did not demonstrate that the trial date should be advanced. The last day of trial is May 29, 2015. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Erin F. Robinson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 12199 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction Prohibiting The Destruction Of Relevant Evidence By Aerojet Rocketdyne, Inc. (Fka Aerojet General Corporation) And Gencorp, Inc.
Off Calendar May-20-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Anne T. Acuna of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Dale Stein, et al. (in #276187); Wayne Runnestrand, et al. (in #276211)
Daniel L. Martens of DAL SOGLIO & MARTENS LLP (310-662-4748), appearing for 3rd party / opposing party Aeroject rocketdyne, Inc. (FKA Aeroject General Corporation); and 3rd party / joinder GenCorp., Inc.
Jeff Coons of GORDON & REES LLP (415-986-5900), appearing for defendant Hitco Carbon Composites, Inc. in the Runnestrand case only

RULING:
BRAYTON PURCELL PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION PROHIBITING THE DESTRUCTION OF RELEVANT EVIDENCE BY AEROJET ROCKETDYNE, INC. (FKA AEROJET GENERAL CORPORATION) AND GENCORP, INC.

Hearing held. The Court took Brayton Purcell LLP plaintiffs' motion for preliminary injunction prohibiting the destruction of relevant evidence by Aerojet Rocketdyne, Inc. (FKA Aerojet General Corporation) and GenCorp, Inc. under submission. Each side submitted a proposed order for the Court's consideration. Judge: Teri L. Jackson Reporter: Tara A. Lohman of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction Prohibiting The Destruction Of Relevant Evidence By Aerojet Rocketdyne, Inc. (Fka Aerojet General Corporation) And Gencorp, Inc.
Off Calendar May-01-2014 Continued To May-07-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for preliminary injunction prohibiting the destruction of relevant evidence by Aerojet Rocketdyne, Inc. and GenCorp, Inc. is continued on the court's own motion to May 7, 2014. No documents may be destroyed until the Court has ruled on this motion as it pertains to this case. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)
